'''Robert James Beswick''' (born 8 December 1984) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} or {{rlp|lf}}. He started his professional career in 2004 with the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. He left the club in 2006 and spent the next ten seasons in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] with the [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]]. In 2017, Beswick joined the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] and played in the club's inaugural league game before finishing his career with [[Newcastle Thunder]]. Beswick also represented at [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] at international level, and was capped 26 times between 2006 and 2019.

==Background==
Beswick was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
====Early career====
Beswick is a former [[Wigan St Patricks]] amateur and Deanery pupil, he played for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s Academy U17s during the 2002 season. He played for the Wigan Academy U21s in 2003, winning the Academy Championship.

====Wigan Warriors====
Beswick was called up into the first team squad for 2004 after the departures of [[Paul Johnson (rugby league born 1978)|Paul Johnson]] and [[Shaun Briscoe]] due to salary cap restrictions. Wigan rugby executive [[Dean Bell]] said: "Bob is an exciting prospect, and his progression through to the first-team squad continues the policy of bringing young players through the ranks."

Beswick made his first team début in [[Terry O'Connor (rugby league footballer)|Terry O'Connor]]'s Testimonial match against [[London Broncos]]. Despite becoming a first team regular during an injury hit 2005 campaign Beswick was released at the end of the season. He had made 22 competitive appearances for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], scoring 2 tries.

====Widnes Vikings====
In October 2005, Bob signed a two-year contract at newly relegated Widnes Vikings.

In his first year with Widnes, Beswick earned a place in the [[Rugby League National Leagues in 2006#National League One|2006 National League One]] Dream Team.<ref name="Bob Beswick">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=1|title=Bob Beswick|publisher=Widnes|date=20 July 2007|accessdate=20 July 2007|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927201123/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=1|archivedate=27 September 2007|url-status=dead|df=dmy }}</ref> 
[[Image:Bob Beswick.jpg|thumb|200px|left|Beswick playing for the Pack]]
He continued to play for Widnes in 2007, scoring 5 tries in 23 games. He signed for Halifax after Widnes lost to Halifax in the [[RFL Championship|National League One]] quarter-final playoff.<ref name="Player Stats">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php|title=Player Stats|publisher=Widnes|date=20 July 2007|accessdate=20 July 2007|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070621200401/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php|archivedate=21 June 2007|url-status=dead|df=dmy }}</ref>

====Halifax====
He signed for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in September 2008 for the 2009 season. Beswick featured on the losing side for Halifax in the 2011 [[Northern Rail Cup]] Final at [[Bloomfield Road]], [[Blackpool]] against [[Leigh Centurions]], before joining Leigh the following year for the 2012 season.

====Leigh Centurions====
While at Leigh, Beswick won the Championship title in [[2014 RFL Championship|2014]] and [[2015 RFL Championship|2015]]. He was named in the Leigh squad for 2016 but did not feature for the club during the season.

====Toronto Wolfpack====
In 2016 it was announced that he would join [[Toronto Wolfpack]] for their inaugural season in 2017, also becoming the club's head of strength and conditioning.

[[Image:Bob Beswick (10 November 2008) 2.jpg|thumb|right|Bob Beswick playing for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] at the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]]]]

===International career===
Beswick is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international.<ref name="White Eyes a Green Jersey">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/07/30/white-eyes-a-green-jersey.aspx|title=White Eyes a Green Jersey|publisher=RLWC08.com|date=21 July 2008|accessdate=30 July 2008 }}{{dead link|date=December 2016|bot=InternetArchiveBot|fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref>

He was named in the Ireland squad as captain for the 2009 European Nations Cup.

At the end of the 2014 season, Bob captained Ireland in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]] tournament.

At the conclusion of the 2015 season, he was the vice-captain of Ireland in the [[2015 European Cup]] tournament.

In 2016 he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref>
